sections.paragraph:
χαλκεύω (χαλκός) pf. pass. ptc. κεχαλκευμένος (Hom. et al.; 1 Km 13:20; Jos., Ant. 3, 172, C. Ap. 2, 242; Ar. 10, 1; Tat.; Ath. 15, 2) to work at a forge, forge, first bronze, then metal gener. (Jos., Bell. 7, 429 a golden lamp) Dg 2:2f.—DELG s.v. χαλκός.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

gener. gener. = generally

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1076
